question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Rounds?
description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined rounds played by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined rounds played by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined rounds" is the sum of rounds won by both teams during the match, including any overtime rounds if applicable. For example, a match ending 13–11 would have a total of 24 rounds (even), while a match ending 13–10 would have a total of 23 rounds (odd).

If Map 1 is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Counter-Strike: Alliance vs ReThink (BO3) - Journey Group D
events.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike match between Alliance and ReThink in the Journey Group D, initially scheduled for March 26 at 7:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Alliance" if Alliance win the match against ReThink.

This market will resolve to "ReThink" if ReThink win the match against Alliance.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: Alliance enters the BO3 matchup against ReThink in Journey Group D as clear favorites, with trader consensus implying around 75% win probability based on recent form and head-to-head edges. Alliance's riflers, led by ALLEYZYNTK, have dominated tier-2 events with a 70% win rate over the last month, including a strong Ancient performance in qualifiers. ReThink struggles with consistency, posting a 3-7 record in similar group stages, hampered by inconsistent CT-side execution. No major roster changes or bans reported; watch for Alliance's map pool advantage on Mirage and Inferno. Momentum favors the Swedes amid ReThink's fatigue from a packed schedule, though upsets loom in volatile CS2 group play.

question: Map 2: Odd/Even Total Kills?
description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 2 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 2 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 2, or if Map 2 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 2 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

This market will resolve to "EYEBALLERS" if EYEBALLERS win the match against Young Ninjas.

This market will resolve to "Young Ninjas" if Young Ninjas win the match against EYEBALLERS.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: EYEBALLERS enter their BO3 clash with Young Ninjas in Journey Group D as clear trader favorites, driven by superior recent form including a 70% win rate over last 10 maps versus Young Ninjas' 40% slump marked by early surrenders on Inferno. Head-to-head favors EYEBALLERS 2-0 historically, amplified by home-server edge and Group D elimination stakes; watch pistol round dominance and Mirage vetoes as key swing factors in this qualifier pressure cooker.

question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Rounds?
description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined rounds played by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined rounds played by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined rounds" is the sum of rounds won by both teams during the match, including any overtime rounds if applicable. For example, a match ending 13–11 would have a total of 24 rounds (even), while a match ending 13–10 would have a total of 23 rounds (odd).

If Map 1 is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: AD Pasto vs. CD Tolima
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Colombia Primera A game, scheduled for Saturday, April 11, 2026 between AD Pasto and CD Tolima.

eventMetadata.context_description: The razor-thin odds gap reflects a classic high-stakes Colombian Primera A showdown, where AD Pasto's formidable home advantage at altitude-heavy Estadio Departamental Libertad counters CD Tolima's edge in recent form and league positioning. Pasto boasts an unbeaten streak in their last five home matches, conceding just three goals, while Tolima rides a three-game away win run with a stingy defense allowing under one goal per outing. Official injury reports show both squads largely intact—no key absences from starters—with head-to-head history split evenly (three wins apiece in last six). Trader consensus stays bunched amid rest advantages for both and playoff implications, highlighting the wisdom of crowds in this toss-up.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both AD Ceuta FC and Cádiz CF each score at least one goal during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if either team fails to score (i.e., if one or both teams finish with zero goals).

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on laliga.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

Momentum tilts toward a competitive, high-scoring affair.

question: Will Argentina’s monthly inflation in March 2026 be less than or equal to 2.1%?
description:
This is a market about the monthly variation of consumer prices in Argentina, before seasonal adjustment, as reported by the National Institute of Statistics and Census (INDEC) of Argentina.

This market will resolve according to the monthly percentage change in the Consumer Price Index (CPI / IPC) in March 2026 (Variación % mensual Total nacional), according to the monthly INDEC report.

The resolution source for this market will be the INDEC Consumer Price Index report released for March 2026 (https://www.indec.gob.ar/), currently scheduled to be released on April 14, 2026. 

Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

You can find this report by clicking on the “Precios al Consumidor” option on the home page of https://www.indec.gob.ar/, and searching the PDF for the figure under “Variación % mensual Total nacional”.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly INDEC CPI (IPC) news release, which reports monthly inflation change to only one decimal point (e.g., 1.9%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders are closely split between 2.8–3.0% (33.5%) and 3.1–3.3% (31.5%) for Argentina's March monthly CPI, mirroring economist consensus forecasts around 3.1% from recent Bloomberg and Reuters polls, as disinflation persists under President Milei's fiscal austerity and monetary tightening. February's 2.4% print—down from 2.7% in December—bolsters optimism for sub-3% territory, but competitive dynamics hinge on regulated price adjustments like energy and transport, which could push toward higher bins if carryover effects emerge. The 2.5–2.7% camp (24%) bets on accelerated slowdown from peso stability and surplus budgets, while tail risks above 3.7% reflect food volatility; watch INDEC's April 14 release for resolution.

question: Inter Kashi FC vs. Mohammedan SC: O/U 3.5
description:
In the upcoming Indian Super League game between Inter Kashi FC and Mohammedan SC, scheduled for April 12 at 7:30 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Inter Kashi FC and Mohammedan SC combine to score 4 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 4, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on indiansuperleague.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Tianjin Jinmen Hu FC vs. Qingdao Hainiu FC
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Chinese Super League game, scheduled for Sunday, April 12, 2026 between Tianjin Jinmen Hu FC and Qingdao Hainiu FC.

eventMetadata.context_description: Tianjin Jinmen Tiger's slim home edge anchors their shared lead at 48.5% alongside the draw in this Chinese Super League clash with Qingdao Hainiu, as trader consensus highlights evenly matched mid-table sides battling relegation pressures. Recent form keeps it razor-tight: Tianjin salvaged a 1-1 draw at home versus Meizhou Hakka last weekend, extending an unbeaten run in four of five, while Hainiu stunned Shanghai SIPG 1-0 away for back-to-back wins, boosting defensive resolve under new coach. No major injuries reported on official lists, but Tianjin's forward Andrea Compagno faces fitness doubts per beat reports, potentially tilting toward Hainiu's counter-threat and historical stalemates—three draws in last five head-to-heads. Momentum and home crowd slightly favor the hosts, yet Hainiu's grit sustains the deadlock.

events.title: Beijing Guoan FC vs. Chengdu Rongcheng FC
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Chinese Super League game, scheduled for Sunday, April 12, 2026 between Beijing Guoan FC and Chengdu Rongcheng FC.

eventMetadata.context_description: Beijing Guoan FC holds a razor-thin edge as slight home favorites at 49.5% implied probability against Chengdu Rongcheng FC's 48.5%, with the draw at 48.0%, reflecting a high-stakes Chinese Super League clash between third- and second-place rivals separated by just two points after 22 rounds. As an Emperor's Cup third-round matchup, historical upsets by lower-division sides like Giravanz add uncertainty, with the hosts unbeaten in four home games this season while Sagan struggles away, conceding two-plus goals in recent outings. No major injuries reported on either side per official updates, but Tosu's fatigue from a packed J1 schedule versus Giravanz's rest edge keeps trader consensus tightly bunched, reflecting the wisdom of crowds on this classic cup dynamic where momentum trumps league pedigree.
